title: "Alter? When The Hills Do."
content:
Alter? When the hills do.
    Falter? When the sun
    Question if his glory
    Be the perfect one.

    Surfeit? When the daffodil
    Doth of the dew:
    Even as herself, O friend!
    I will of you!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled ""Alter? When The Hills Do."", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
